notes: Three search piles at The Mixing Glass. Items are the small tools of the bar — they don't change the world but the noticing changes Frank.

the_jigger.label: The bar jigger
the_jigger.flavor: Two-sided. Ounce-and-a-quarter on the high side, three-quarter on the low. The high side is worn smoother — that's the side Maddie uses most.

the_mixing_glass.label: The mixing glass
the_mixing_glass.flavor: The bar's namesake. Etched on the bottom: HAWTHORNE STRAINER MFG. CO. 1939. The glass is older than everybody in the building.

house_bitters.label: House bitters dropper
house_bitters.flavor: Maddie made these in November. Cardamom and orange peel and one secret thing. The bottle has no label.

a_matchbook.label: A matchbook (THE MIXING GLASS)
a_matchbook.flavor: Black on cream, the bar's logo in tasteful slant. Three matches left in the book. Most of them have been struck.

a_used_cocktail_napkin.label: A used cocktail napkin
a_used_cocktail_napkin.flavor: Folded, then re-folded, then set down. Phone number on it in pencil. Half a phone number. The other half is on a napkin you'll never find.

an_olive_pick.label: An olive pick
an_olive_pick.flavor: Brass, branded. The booth had four pickled olives at the start of the date and zero at the end. The pick was, somewhere in there, set aside.

the_empty_keg.label: An empty keg
the_empty_keg.flavor: In the back hall, on the dolly, waiting for tomorrow's delivery. The keg is empty of beer and full of the room's air. The smell is good.

the_shift_drink_log.label: The shift-drink log
the_shift_drink_log.flavor: Maddie writes her shift drink in a small book on the lip of the dish-pit. Tonight's entry, when you flip to it later, reads: nothing.

the_bar_apron.label: A bar apron, folded
the_bar_apron.flavor: Not Maddie's. The closing apron. Black with a small stain near the hem from a glass of red. Folded the way the closing apron always gets folded.
